task: chapter-3 corrected frontier ridge splitbookend batch

hypotheses_considered.summary: Hold the successful anchor7 negative corridor bookend fixed and locally refine only the anchor12 terminal y reclosure dwell around the current y1 splitbookend runner-up, to test whether the strong median gain can be retained while recovering mean/max.
Hold the terminal y1 closure fixed and micro-perturb only the anchor7 negative corridor dwell to see whether the runner-up sits on a narrow or broad corridor-dose ridge.

selected_families: splitbookend terminal-y ridge on corrected entry backbone
splitbookend corridor-dose microcheck on corrected entry backbone

rows_sorted.rationale: Keep the terminal y1 closure fixed and slightly strengthen the anchor7 negative corridor dwell to 1.5 s.
Reduce only the anchor12 terminal y dwell from 1.0 s to 0.5 s while keeping the successful anchor7 negative corridor conditioner fixed.
Midpoint terminal-y refinement between the current y1 runner-up and the lighter y0.5 closure.
Keep the terminal y1 closure fixed and lighten only the anchor7 negative corridor dwell to 0.5 s.
Slight terminal-y dose-up around the current runner-up, to verify whether y1 is already near the useful edge or still under-driven.

bottom_line.statement: The splitbookend ridge improved the runner-up branch on mean and max, but not enough to overtake the incumbent or the best max-oriented front-z side branch.
